title: Unified Paper 55: Higgs and Vacuum 3 — Vacuum Stability

d_claims.claim: The vacuum stability requires λ > 0 at all scales. The SM renormalization group flow gives λ → 0 at ~10^10 GeV (the metastability scale).
d_claims.evidence: Degrassi et al. 2012, Bednyakov et al. 2015; Theorem 55.1

d_claims.claim: The metastability scale is ~10^10 GeV, far above the electroweak scale (174 GeV) but far below the Planck scale (10^19 GeV).

The SM vacuum is metastable: the Higgs potential is bounded from below at the electroweak scale but becomes unbounded at high scales. The metastability is a consequence of the top quark Yukawa coupling.
The lifetime of the metastable vacuum exceeds the age of the universe (~13.8 Gyr), so the vacuum is effectively stable for all practical purposes.

In the FLCR framework, the metastability is a boundary repair phenomenon (Paper 5): the electroweak scale is the "repaired" boundary where λ > 0; the high-scale region is the "unrepaired" region where λ < 0.
The boundary repair operation (Paper 5) is the RG flow that keeps λ positive up to the metastability scale. The repair curvature K(v) is the RG flow of λ.
